---
title: "Code text"
---

The coding screen is built around three areas: the codebook, the source text, and the applied-code bar.

## Navigate sources

Use the source grid to choose a source. Within a source, move through sentences and passages with the keyboard or mouse.

## Apply codes

Select the passage you want to code, then apply a code from the codebook. ACE supports keyboard-driven coding so repeated actions do not require moving back and forth between panels.

If the same code is applied to overlapping text, ACE merges the overlapping spans into a single annotation for that code.

## Review applied codes

The applied-code bar shows which codes are attached to the current passage. Use it to check whether a passage has been coded as intended.

## Undo and redo

Use undo and redo while coding to recover from recent changes. Undo history is kept for the open project during the current app session.
